name: waterfall-chart
description: Evaluate and improve waterfall chart slides — start, drivers, and total with clear gain/loss logic
goal: Achieve consistent 12+/14 with complete driver sequencing, crisp start-to-finish logic, and CFO-grade proof

prompts.text: Build a waterfall slide showing how EBITDA moved from $42M to $57M via price +8M, volume +5M, mix +4M, inflation -2M
Build a waterfall slide showing how a cost program delivered $18M savings from procurement 7M, automation 5M, footprint 4M, overhead 2M
Build a waterfall slide bridging revenue from plan $120M to actual $128M via price, volume, churn, and upsell
Build a waterfall slide showing cash bridge from EBITDA to free cash flow with tax, capex, working capital, and restructuring items
Build a waterfall slide showing headcount change from 380 to 450 with hiring, attrition, acquisition, and reorg effects
Build a waterfall slide showing carbon reduction from 100 to 60 units via efficiency, renewables, and offsets
Build a waterfall slide showing gross margin expansion from 42% to 49% with pricing, mix, productivity, and input cost effects
Build a waterfall slide showing incident reduction from 120 to 48 via observability, automation, patching, and training
Build a waterfall slide showing merger synergy build-up from baseline to $65M run-rate synergy target
Build a waterfall slide showing ROI bridge from investment to value captured across revenue, savings, and productivity drivers
